creator: philip@FreeBSD.org
text:
This is not a bug.

Once upon a time we distributed distfiles on ftp-archive.  Originally when we moved them to our CDN, we had the directory index enabled.  We soon turned that off because it's very computationally expensive and third-parties were using it to populate very inefficient and perpetually out-of-date "mirrors".

We should either annotate that reference to distcache with "note: no directory index", or simply stop referring to it.  Or simply remove ports/distfiles from ftp-archive.

I'll mark this as "not a bug", but I'm open to suggestions about the best way forward.

(Replacing a broken configuration (for many years now) referring to ftp-archive with one referring to distcache still works.  Only indexing is turned off.)
